title: Brickellia subuligera
taxonomy: Brickellia subuligera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Brickellia, which comprises around 40 species of shrubs and small trees. The genus Brickellia is further divided into several sections, and Brickellia subuligera is thought to be part of the section Brickellia. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. The specific classification of Brickellia subuligera is still subject to ongoing research and confirmation by botanists.

intro: Brickellia subuligera, also known as "Little Barren Brickellia" or "Mexican Brickellia", is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Brickellia subuligera include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to harness its healing potential.
